verdict_reasoning: ABCB9 (TAPL) is a half-transporter ABC family member that localizes to lysosomal membranes, where it transports polypeptides from cytosol into the lysosomal lumen. Not cytoplasmic (membrane protein, ruled out). Not cell_state_induced — no documented stress/activation-induced PM display. Not tissue_restricted_surface — broadly expressed in lysosomes, no specialized lineage surface display. Not lysosomal_exocytosis — while it is lysosomal, there is no documented PM appearance via lysosomal fusion as a therapeutic-relevant pool. Not dual_localization — steady-state is lysosomal, no documented PM pool. Not stable_surface_attachment — not secreted. Best fit: endomembrane_resident (lysosomal).
predicted_key_uncertainty: Lysosomal exocytosis could transiently expose ABCB9 at PM with lumenal loops outward, but no gene-specific evidence supports this as accessible.
